title: Thalkirchen-Obersendling-Forstenried-Fürstenried-Solln

infobox.value: (German pronunciation: [[taːlˈkɪʁçn̩ oːbɐˈzɛntlɪŋ ˈfɔʁstn̩ˌʁiːt ˈfʏʁstn̩ˌʁiːt ˈzɔl̩n]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German"); [Central Bavarian](/wiki/Central_Bavarian "Central Bavarian"): *Thoikircha-Obasendling-Forstnriad-Fiastnriad-Soin*) is the [19th borough](/wiki/Boroughs_of_Munich "Boroughs of Munich") of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"), [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"), comprising the extreme southern part of the city on the west bank of the river [Isar](/wiki/Isar "Isar"). After the administrative reform, the boroughs Thalkirchen-Obersendling-Forstenried and Solln were incorporated, thus forming the new borough. It comprises 17.7631 square kilometres and has a population of 96,714 residents (as of 2018).[[1]](https://www.muenchen.de/rathaus/dam/jcr:1c6b38f7-5e7e-4114-bde8-5ca74200c561/LHM-StatTB_2019.pdf)

is located on the river Isar and is a mostly residential district. Sights include the Flaucher, a part of the Isarauen ("floodplains") and a popular recreation site, and [Tierpark Hellabrunn](/wiki/Tierpark_Hellabrunn "Tierpark Hellabrunn") (Munich Zoo) which can be found on the opposite side of the river from Thalkirchen. The U-Bahn station [Thalkirchen](/wiki/Thalkirchen_(Munich_U-Bahn) "Thalkirchen (Munich U-Bahn)") provides westside riverfront access to the zoo, which is on the eastern bank of the river. This is possible because of the Tierpark bridge, connecting the two sides, which is very close to the location of the U-Bahn station.

is located up-slope from Thalkirchen and north of Solln. The district is dominated by [Siemens](/wiki/Siemens "Siemens") which has a large presence at [Siemenswerke](/w/index.php?title=Siemenswerke&action=edit&redlink=1 "Siemenswerke (page does not exist)"). The district can be reached by U-Bahn via the [Aidenbachstraße](/wiki/Aidenbachstra%C3%9Fe_(Munich_U-Bahn) "Aidenbachstraße (Munich U-Bahn)") and [Obersendling](/wiki/Obersendling_(Munich_U-Bahn) "Obersendling (Munich U-Bahn)") stations, which are on the U3 line. It is also served by the [München Siemenswerke station](/wiki/M%C3%BCnchen_Siemenswerke_station "München Siemenswerke station"), which is on the S7, S20, and S27 S-Bahn lines.
is an area in the far southwest of the city of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"). It is mostly residential, with some shopping, restaurant, and entertainment opportunities.

is a residential neighborhood along the [A95](/wiki/Bundesautobahn_95 "Bundesautobahn 95") that was initiated in 1959 as a large scale urban housing development by the municipality and the savings bank of Munich [Stadtsparkasse München](/wiki/Stadtsparkasse_M%C3%BCnchen "Stadtsparkasse München").

is a mostly upscale, low-density residential area, recognized as one of the nicest in Munich, which includes the southernmost land area in the city of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"). Solln is close to, but not adjacent to the River [Isar](/wiki/Isar "Isar"), which is just to the east. Solln is served by [München Solln station](/wiki/M%C3%BCnchen_Solln_station "München Solln station"), which is on the [S7](/wiki/S7_(Munich) "S7 (Munich)") and [S20](/wiki/S20_(Munich) "S20 (Munich)") S-Bahn lines.

outgoing_links.target_title: Maximilian II Emanuel, Elector of Bavaria

[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [48°05′20″N 11°31′08″E﻿ / ﻿48.089°N 11.519°E﻿ / 48.089; 11.519](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Thalkirchen-Obersendling-Forstenried-F%C3%BCrstenried-Solln&params=48.089_N_11.519_E_source:dewiki)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Borough of Munich, Germany

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/be/M%C3%BCnchen_-_Stadtbezirk_19_%28Karte%29_-_Thalkirchen_-_Obersendling_-_Forstenried_-_F%C3%BCrstenried_-_Solln.png/250px-M%C3%BCnchen_-_Stadtbezirk_19_%28Karte%29_-_Thalkirchen_-_Obersendling_-_Forstenried_-_F%C3%BCrstenried_-_Solln.png)](/wiki/File:M%C3%BCnchen_-_Stadtbezirk_19_(Karte)_-_Thalkirchen_-_Obersendling_-_Forstenried_-_F%C3%BCrstenried_-_Solln.png)

19th Borough, location within Munich

**Thalkirchen-Obersendling-Forstenried-Fürstenried-Solln** (German pronunciation: [[taːlˈkɪʁçn̩ oːbɐˈzɛntlɪŋ ˈfɔʁstn̩ˌʁiːt ˈfʏʁstn̩ˌʁiːt ˈzɔl̩n]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German"); [Central Bavarian](/wiki/Central_Bavarian "Central Bavarian"): *Thoikircha-Obasendling-Forstnriad-Fiastnriad-Soin*) is the [19th borough](/wiki/Boroughs_of_Munich "Boroughs of Munich") of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"), [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"), comprising the extreme southern part of the city on the west bank of the river [Isar](/wiki/Isar "Isar"). After the administrative reform, the boroughs Thalkirchen-Obersendling-Forstenried and Solln were incorporated, thus forming the new borough. It comprises 17.7631 square kilometres and has a population of 96,714 residents (as of 2018).[[1]](https://www.muenchen.de/rathaus/dam/jcr:1c6b38f7-5e7e-4114-bde8-5ca74200c561/LHM-StatTB_2019.pdf)

## Subdivisions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thalkirchen-Obersendling-Forstenried-F%C3%BCrstenried-Solln&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Subdivisions")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/15/20140208_obersendling_2.JPG/250px-20140208_obersendling_2.JPG)](/wiki/File:20140208_obersendling_2.JPG)

Residential area in Obersendling

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f1/Thalkirchner_Bruecke_Muenchen-1.jpg/250px-Thalkirchner_Bruecke_Muenchen-1.jpg)](/wiki/File:Thalkirchner_Bruecke_Muenchen-1.jpg)

Thalkirchner bridge crossing the [Isar](/wiki/Isar "Isar")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/32/Sparkassen-Hochhaus_F%C3%BCrstenried_1.jpg/250px-Sparkassen-Hochhaus_F%C3%BCrstenried_1.jpg)](/wiki/File:Sparkassen-Hochhaus_F%C3%BCrstenried_1.jpg)

Sparkassenhochhaus Fürstenried 1

### Thalkirchen

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thalkirchen-Obersendling-Forstenried-F%C3%BCrstenried-Solln&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Thalkirchen")]

**Thalkirchen** is located on the river Isar and is a mostly residential district. Sights include the Flaucher, a part of the Isarauen ("floodplains") and a popular recreation site, and [Tierpark Hellabrunn](/wiki/Tierpark_Hellabrunn "Tierpark Hellabrunn") (Munich Zoo) which can be found on the opposite side of the river from Thalkirchen. The U-Bahn station [Thalkirchen](/wiki/Thalkirchen_(Munich_U-Bahn) "Thalkirchen (Munich U-Bahn)") provides westside riverfront access to the zoo, which is on the eastern bank of the river. This is possible because of the Tierpark bridge, connecting the two sides, which is very close to the location of the U-Bahn station.

### Obersendling

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thalkirchen-Obersendling-Forstenried-F%C3%BCrstenried-Solln&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Obersendling")]

**Obersendling** is located up-slope from Thalkirchen and north of Solln. The district is dominated by [Siemens](/wiki/Siemens "Siemens") which has a large presence at [Siemenswerke](/w/index.php?title=Siemenswerke&action=edit&redlink=1 "Siemenswerke (page does not exist)"). The district can be reached by U-Bahn via the [Aidenbachstraße](/wiki/Aidenbachstra%C3%9Fe_(Munich_U-Bahn) "Aidenbachstraße (Munich U-Bahn)") and [Obersendling](/wiki/Obersendling_(Munich_U-Bahn) "Obersendling (Munich U-Bahn)") stations, which are on the U3 line. It is also served by the [München Siemenswerke station](/wiki/M%C3%BCnchen_Siemenswerke_station "München Siemenswerke station"), which is on the S7, S20, and S27 S-Bahn lines.

### Forstenried

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thalkirchen-Obersendling-Forstenried-F%C3%BCrstenried-Solln&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Forstenried")]

**Forstenried** is an area in the far southwest of the city of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"). It is mostly residential, with some shopping, restaurant, and entertainment opportunities.

Forstenried is located west of [Solln](/wiki/Solln "Solln") and [Obersendling](/wiki/Obersendling "Obersendling"), east of [Fürstenried](/wiki/F%C3%BCrstenried "Fürstenried"), south of [Hadern](/wiki/Hadern "Hadern"), south of [Sendling-Westpark](/wiki/Sendling-Westpark "Sendling-Westpark"), and north of Forstenrieder Park (gemeindefrei). It is more specifically bounded by the A95 to the west, Drygalski Allee and Waterloostraße to the east, Boschetsriederstraße to the north, and Forstenrieder Park to the south. It is served by the U3 U-bahn stations [Forstenrieder Allee](/wiki/Forstenrieder_Allee_(Munich_U-Bahn) "Forstenrieder Allee (Munich U-Bahn)") and [Basler Straße](/wiki/Basler_Stra%C3%9Fe_(Munich_U-Bahn) "Basler Straße (Munich U-Bahn)"). Buslines include buses 132, 133, and 134.

Forstenried is centered on the intersection of Forstenrieder Allee and Liesl-Karlstadt-Straße/ Herterichstraße. This is the center of the old village of Forstenried, which was incorporated as part of Munich in 1912. The Heilig Kreuz (Holy Cross) Church is at the center of the old village, as well as the [Maypole](/wiki/Maypole "Maypole"), a [World War I](/wiki/World_War_I "World War I") and [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II") memorial, some shops, a pharmacy, a social club, and some restaurants.

Forstenried abuts Forstenrieder Park, a large, protected forest, stretching to the south toward [Pullach](/wiki/Pullach "Pullach") and [Starnberg](/wiki/Starnberg "Starnberg") and to the west [Neuried](/wiki/Neuried,_Bavaria "Neuried, Bavaria"). Forstenrieder Park offers recreational opportunities, with paved and unpaved paths, including [deciduous](/wiki/Deciduous "Deciduous") and [evergreen trees](/wiki/Evergreen_trees "Evergreen trees"). Forstenried borders a major European headquarters for [McDonald's](/wiki/McDonald%27s "McDonald's"). On the northeast side, just outside Forstenried, near McDonald's, there is a large retail area, including car dealerships, fitness centers, a major hardware store, a major electronics store, and more. On the northwestern side, there are shopping opportunities centered on the Forstenrieder Allee U-Bahn station, including a bowling alley. The area north and west of Zuricherstraße includes many high-density apartments, whereas heading south on Forstenrieder Allee from Forstenrieder Allee U-Bahn station there is mostly lower-density housing. The area south of the center of Forstenried, heading toward Forstenrieder Park, include mostly upscale, single-family housing. The area north and east of Forstenrieder Allee U-Bahn station is mostly lower-density housing. On the far southeast side, adjacent to Drygalski Alee and Herterichstraße, there are some high-rise apartments, bordered by large crop fields. This area includes one large apartment building that is over 20 stories tall and serves as a major landmark, as one of the tallest apartments in Munich and the tallest building in southern Munich.

Forstenried is served by Bad Forstenrieder Park, a public bath. It is also served by Tennis Forstenrieder Park. A shooting range is located on the border of Forstenrieder Park, at the Verein Hubertus f. Jagd- u. Sportschießen e.V. The Bezirkssportanlage Solln (a sports club) and the surrounding public park provide recreational opportunities to Forstenried residents on the southside of the district, including a dirt bike park, a view of the Alps from an artificial hill, playgrounds, and fruit-picking opportunities in the surrounding fields.

The A95 provides easy access to [Lake Starnberg](/wiki/Lake_Starnberg "Lake Starnberg"), just ten minutes to the southwest, as well as to recreational opportunities in the [Bavarian Alps](/wiki/Bavarian_Alps "Bavarian Alps"), including the ski town of [Garmisch-Partenkirchen](/wiki/Garmisch-Partenkirchen "Garmisch-Partenkirchen"). The A95 also allows residents to quickly access central [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"), including the [Mittlerer Ring](/wiki/Mittlerer_Ring "Mittlerer Ring"), where the autobahn terminates.

### Fürstenried

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thalkirchen-Obersendling-Forstenried-F%C3%BCrstenried-Solln&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Fürstenried")]

**Fürstenried** is a residential neighborhood along the [A95](/wiki/Bundesautobahn_95 "Bundesautobahn 95") that was initiated in 1959 as a large scale urban housing development by the municipality and the savings bank of Munich [Stadtsparkasse München](/wiki/Stadtsparkasse_M%C3%BCnchen "Stadtsparkasse München").

Fürstenried was developed in three discrete construction phases: Fürstenried 1 (now Fürstenried Ost), Fürstenried 2 (now Fürstenried West), and Fürstenried 3 (now Neu-Forstenried)

#### Fürstenried 1

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thalkirchen-Obersendling-Forstenried-F%C3%BCrstenried-Solln&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Fürstenried 1")]

Now **Fürstenried Ost**, was built from 1960-1961 after an urban planning commission chose the development proposal by Architects Franz Ruf and Fred Angerer. It is located to the east of the Bundesautobahn A95. At the center of the development is the Sparkassenhochhaus, a 14 story residential building in the style of a slab high rise built in 1961 from plans of the Studio of the German Architect Fred Angerer by the architects Kurt Räder and Gerhard Feuser. The building was awarded the first prize of the  [Association of German Architects BDA](/wiki/Association_of_German_Architects "Association of German Architects") in Bavaria in 1967.[[1]](#cite_note-1) At the time of completion, it was the largest single residential building in Bavaria.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

#### Fürstenried 2

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thalkirchen-Obersendling-Forstenried-F%C3%BCrstenried-Solln&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Fürstenried 2")]

Now **Fürstenried West**, was also part of the original development plan and features groups of single family homes in a low-rise high-density configuration erected in 1962-1963.

#### Fürstenried 3

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thalkirchen-Obersendling-Forstenried-F%C3%BCrstenried-Solln&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Fürstenried 3")]

Now **Neu-Forstenried**, is a similar density extension of the development Fürstenried Ost and was erected after 1963. It is situated to the south of Fürstenried West alongside the Autobahn and adjacent to the Forstenrieder Park.

The development is named after [Schloss Fürstenried](/wiki/Schloss_Fuerstenried "Schloss Fuerstenried"), a palace erected in 1715 by [Max Emanuel, Elector of Bavaria](/wiki/Maximilian_II_Emanuel,_Elector_of_Bavaria "Maximilian II Emanuel, Elector of Bavaria") as a hunting lodge.

### Solln

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thalkirchen-Obersendling-Forstenried-F%C3%BCrstenried-Solln&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Solln")]

**Solln** is a mostly upscale, low-density residential area, recognized as one of the nicest in Munich, which includes the southernmost land area in the city of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"). Solln is close to, but not adjacent to the River [Isar](/wiki/Isar "Isar"), which is just to the east. Solln is served by [München Solln station](/wiki/M%C3%BCnchen_Solln_station "München Solln station"), which is on the [S7](/wiki/S7_(Munich) "S7 (Munich)") and [S20](/wiki/S20_(Munich) "S20 (Munich)") S-Bahn lines.
